context_confidence_notes: Very high confidence. All task definitions clear, dependencies explicit, story notes provide recommended sequential ordering. Confidence limited only by lack of empirical verification of intake agent behavior (T3) and downstream stage behavior (T4)—but these are designed as de-risking tasks.

uows_scheduled.title: Verify and document fixture validation and error handling
Fix and verify workflow routing for synthetic mode
Verify intake agent creates canonical artifacts for synthetic stories
uows_scheduled.risk_reason: Intake agent behavior is unverified; artifact structure is critical for downstream stages

uows_scheduled.title: Verify downstream stages skip ADO operations for synthetic artifacts
uows_scheduled.risk_reason: Downstream stage synthetic mode detection unverified; AC3 depends on this

uows_scheduled.title: Add comprehensive integration test for full synthetic workflow
uows_scheduled.risk_reason: Integration test is gate for all three acceptance criteria; high complexity exercises all components

uows_scheduled.title: Document synthetic workflow usage and behavior
uows_scheduled.special_notes: Documentation task; synthesizes findings from T1-T5

parallel_batches.rationale: T1 (fixture validation) and T2 (workflow routing) have no shared file modifications or interdependent logic. Both are unit-level tests with no side effects.

critical_path.rationale: Longest dependency chain: T1 → T3 → T4 → T5. T2 runs in parallel with T1, not extending critical path. T6 is off critical path (low priority documentation).

scheduling_decisions.rationale: Both tasks are independent with no shared concerns. Parallelization saves execution time without risk.

scheduling_decisions.decision: T3 in batch 2 (immediately after T1, T2)
scheduling_decisions.rationale: Logically follows foundation work. Acts as gate for artifact structure verification. Critical for AC2, AC3.
Explicitly depends on T3 artifacts. Verifies downstream stage behavior—architectural requirement for AC3.
Integration test depends on T1, T2, T3 being verified. Scheduled after T4 to benefit from downstream stage verification.
Documentation task with low priority. Depends on T2, T3. Runs last after verification complete. Summarizes findings from T1-T5.

scheduling_decisions.decision: Risk de-risking: T2 scheduled in batch 1
scheduling_decisions.rationale: Test assertion fix (runner parameter) is high-risk item that could break entire test suite. Early scheduling enables fast failure detection.

scheduling_decisions.decision: Risk de-risking: T3 scheduled in batch 2
scheduling_decisions.rationale: Intake agent behavior is unknown and high-risk. Early scheduling enables fast failure detection if artifacts are malformed.

scheduling_decisions.decision: Risk de-risking: T4 scheduled in batch 3
scheduling_decisions.rationale: Downstream stage synthetic mode detection is unknown and high-risk. Early scheduling enables fast failure detection before integration testing.

risk_drivers.mitigation: Scheduled early in batch 2; clear artifact validation in DoD; creates diagnostic failures
Scheduled early in batch 3; unit tests with mocked ADO; integration test confirms no API calls
Simple fix (runner parameter); scheduled in batch 1 for early validation

confidence_limiting_factors: Intake agent implementation unknown—will be clarified during T3
Downstream stage synthetic mode detection not yet verified—will be clarified during T4
Integration test scope requires all prior stages to work—high complexity

notes.story_context: Synthetic workflow smoke test using local fixture instead of Azure DevOps. Three acceptance criteria: (1) workflow starts from local fixture, (2) intake preserves raw input and normalizes artifacts, (3) ADO-specific actions are skipped.
notes.fixture_status: Well-formed fixture exists at agent-context/test-fixtures/synthetic_story.json. No fixture changes needed.
notes.existing_infrastructure: Story notes document that substantial synthetic fixture infrastructure already exists: argument parsing (run.py), validation (workflow_inputs.py), routing (resolve_workflow_input), prompt generation (build_intake_prompt), test cases (9 tests in test_workflow_inputs.py). Only 1 test failure: test_main_defaults_to_bundled_synthetic_fixture needs assertion fix.
notes.schedule_rationale: Based on task story notes which recommend sequential order: T1/T2 → T3 → T4 → T5 → T6. This order respects all explicit dependencies while maximizing safe parallelization and de-risking high-risk items early.
notes.no_unknown_dependencies: All 6 tasks have been analyzed. Task dependencies are explicit in tasks.yaml. No hidden or inferred dependencies discovered.
notes.assignment_scope: Assignments created for all 6 UoWs. All mapped to software-engineer role (story does not indicate need for other roles). All DoD items specified in task definitions are sufficient for acceptance.

queries_made.result: Protocol documentation retrieved. Referenced for lesson retrieval and knowledge gateway approach.

knowledge_sources.usage: Primary source for task definitions, dependencies, DoD items, and recommended sequential ordering
Story context, acceptance criteria, constraints, raw fixture input
Intake constraints, fixture context, ADO metadata absence confirmation

output_artifacts_created.summary: Execution schedule with 5 batches, 6 UoWs, 1 parallelization opportunity, critical path identification, risk ordering, and metacognitive context

quality_checks.check_description: All explicit task dependencies respected. No circular dependencies. T1, T2 have no dependencies. T3 depends on T1, T2 (logical foundation). T4 depends on T3. T5 depends on T1, T2, T3. T6 depends on T2, T3.
